## Chronology Spine

## Core Truth Set
- (5/5) Authority is reframed as alignment-organizing force rather than position, dominance, or command volume. | Quote: "True power does not force-it organizes."
- (5/5) Decision filter evolves toward identifying what keeps the whole space from breaking, emphasizing stabilizers over incidents. | Quote: "What holds this entire space together? ... what keeps all of this from breaking?"
- (5/5) Primary perception mode shifts to simultaneous multi-system awareness across group style, spacing, and authority expression. | Quote: "Multi-System Awareness ... many systems interacting simultaneously."
- (4/5) Blind spot becomes overconfidence that large systems can always stabilize, without yet grasping scale-amplified failure. | Quote: "Believes large systems can always stabilize ... does NOT yet understand: scale can amplify failure."

## Setting Anchors
- Great Council Multi-System Container [FIELD]: Council-scale spatial container where multiple systems coexist without merging and where distance itself carries structural function.

## Continuity Constraints
- Systems Interacting With Systems: Scale-perception constraint.
- Awe-to-Focus Regulation: Affective-regulation constraint.
- Stillness-Center Power Detection: Power-legibility constraint at scale.

## Open Research Gaps
- Which concrete markers best signal multi-system readability versus overload in council-scale scenes? (priority 2): Needed to keep Scene 15 complexity legible while preserving depth.
- How can organizing power be shown consistently without reverting to command-and-dominance tropes? (priority 2): Needed for continuity with Campti-alignment authority model.
- What subtle Scene 15 seeds best foreshadow later scale-amplified failure without collapsing current coherence? (priority 2): Needed to set up Scene 17 while preserving present-stage confidence.
